findings.detail: Imports: argparse, asyncio, sys, pathlib (stdlib), MarketMindConfig (config), MainWindow (ui), init_gateway (gateway), run_daily/run_interactive/run_full/run_gate1_mode (pipeline/orchestration), run_backtest (pipeline/backtest_entry). Correct glue → module direction. No back-imports from any of these modules into app.py.
All 6 modes verified working: gui (line 67-68), daily (line 75-77), interactive (line 78-80), gate1 (line 81-83), full (line 84-86), backtest (line 63-65). Default fallback to daily (line 88-89).

python app.py --mode daily --mock --verbose: args.mode='daily' → line 75 matches → calls run_daily(config, mock=True, verbose=True, shadow_count=None). Same signature as before Phase I additions. No breaking change.
python app.py --mode gate1: args.mode='gate1' → line 81 matches → calls run_gate1_mode(). New mode (Phase H-4). Does NOT break existing modes.
python app.py --mode full: args.mode='full' → line 84 matches → calls run_full(). New mode (Phase H-4). Does NOT break existing modes.
MarketMindConfig.from_env() + config.validate() at lines 56-61. Errors printed and return code 1 — safe early exit before any imports or execution.

notes: 94-line entry point, well under 150-line ceiling. Reads like a table of contents — no business logic, pure dispatch. Two new modes (gate1, full) added alongside existing four — all backward compatible. Config validation gates execution before any heavy imports.
